front.title: Human-AI Relationships
front.subtitle: Exploring the social, ethical, and emotional boundaries between biological and synthetic intelligence.

back.text:
Science fiction has long served as a sandbox for exploring how humans might interact with sentient machines. From Isaac Asimov's positronic robots to the empathetic operating systems in modern cinema, these narratives examine whether artificial intelligence can truly experience emotion or if they merely simulate it to facilitate human cooperation. These stories often mirror contemporary sociological questions regarding labor, personhood, and the nature of companionship.

In technical reality, human-AI interaction is a field of study focused on social robotics and affective computing. Engineers design systems that use natural language processing and facial recognition to mirror human social cues, creating a sense of rapport. While current AI lacks consciousness, the human tendency to anthropomorphize technology suggests that as machines become more lifelike, the psychological and ethical complexity of these relationships will continue to grow.
